{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question is whether SARS-CoV-2 is transmitted through breast milk. Reviewing the provided documents, there is no evidence indicating that SARS-CoV-2 is transmitted through breast milk. Document [2] states: 'Lactating mother: No data has shown any potential adverse effects on the breastfed child.' This implies that transmission through breast milk is not supported by current data. Document [13] emphasizes appropriate lactation practices and does not mention transmission via breast milk. Document [27] mentions QACs in breast milk and blood, but this is unrelated to SARS-CoV-2 transmission. Document [20] discusses HIV transmission through breast milk, which is a different virus and context. There is no document stating that SARS-CoV-2 is present or transmitted in breast milk. Therefore, based on the available evidence, SARS-CoV-2 is not transmitted through breast milk.", "answer_choice": "B"}
